title: Resolve omi-fetch and apps-registry skill/app name collisions (the single worst finding)
prompt:
Two of the four new dark-factory query apps are functionally invisible to natural-language use because an older, pre-existing appydave-plugins skill shares near-identical name and trigger vocabulary:

1. `appydave-plugins/appydave/skills/omi-fetch/SKILL.md` (the old com.appydave.omi-sync-era fetch script) triggers on "what did I record", "omi conversations", "what's in omi" — exactly what a human/agent would say to reach the NEW ~/dev/ad/apps/omi-fetch/ app's codes/tags/synopsis store. skills-registry/SKILL.md (line ~134) only lists the old one.

2. `appydave-plugins/appydave/skills/apps-registry/SKILL.md` (governs ~/.config/appydave/apps.json) triggers on "list apps", "find app", "what apps do I have" — exactly what someone would say to reach the NEW ~/dev/ad/apps/app-registry/ tool's query.py (running/dark/layer/app/stale/drift/all).

This is a naming/discoverability problem across two repos (dark-factory's apps + appydave-plugins' skills), not a code bug in either app. Read both old SKILL.md files and both new apps' READMEs, then propose and execute a disambiguation: e.g. rename one side (skill vs app) to remove the collision, OR add an explicit one-line pointer in each old skill's description ('for the newer, drift-aware registry see ~/dev/ad/apps/app-registry/') so a session that lands on the old skill first still discovers the new one exists. Do not silently delete or disable the old skills — they may still be load-bearing for their own users. Confirm with David before any rename that touches appydave-plugins (a separate, tracked repo) if the safer in-scope fix (adding pointers) doesn't feel sufficient.
verify: Both old SKILL.md files (omi-fetch, apps-registry) carry an explicit, accurate pointer to the newer dark-factory app they might be confused with; skills-registry/SKILL.md's OMI row and any apps-registry row are updated to disambiguate; no old skill functionality is removed or broken.

result.approach: Chose the safer in-scope fix (pointers), not a rename — did not need to escalate to David for confirmation since renaming wasn't required.

appydave-plugins (repo ~/dev/ad/appydave: appydave/skills/omi-fetch/SKILL.md — description gained a disambiguation clause; new '## Related app' section with lookup.py usage; 'Related' bullet list at bottom gained an omi-fetch-app entry
appydave/skills/apps-registry/SKILL.md — description gained a disambiguation clause; new '## Related app' section with query.py verbs; 'Adjacent Skills' list gained an app-registry-app entry

appydave/skills/app-query/SKILL.md — one-line cross-reference added to 'Related Skills' pointing at apps-registry's disambiguation (app-query itself does not collide, added for completeness per ticket wording 'apps-registry/app-query')

appydave/skills/skills-registry/SKILL.md — new 'Known name collisions (skill vs. app, disambiguate — don't merge)' block under the family taxonomy table (~line 152), covering both the omi-fetch and apps-registry cases
omi-fetch (repo ~/dev/ad/apps/omi-fetch,: README.md — new '## A known neighbour: the `appydave:omi-fetch` skill' section (placed beside the existing com.appydave.omi-sync neighbour note), reverse-pointing to the skill for ad-hoc fetches
app-registry (repo ~/dev/ad/apps/app-reg: README.md — new '## A known neighbour: the `appydave:apps-registry` skill' section, reverse-pointing to the skill for registry CRUD
dark-factory (this repo): No CLAUDE.md change made. Checked dark-factory's own CLAUDE.md — it has no 'query tools' section (that section lives only in ~/dev/ad/CLAUDE.md, listing query_brain/query_omi/llm_context, not these two apps). Ticket 20260704T0634Z-claude-md-query-tools-pointer (still in engine/store/queue/, unclaimed) is the correct, broader owner of adding such a section for all four query CLIs — did not partially build it here to avoid conflicting with that ticket's fuller scope. Orchestrator: 20260704T0634Z is NOT closed by this ticket; it still needs its own execution.

routing_sentences_added.omi-fetch skill description (appended): Use for ad-hoc API fetches — for "what came in" / "find the conversation about X" against the already-archived store, an always-on omi-fetch APP also exists, see "Related app" below.
routing_sentences_added.omi-fetch skill body, key line: Use the app's index for "what came in" / "find the conversation about X" / anything already captured — it's the richer, continuously-updated store. Use this skill for ad-hoc, one-off API fetches: a specific date/range not yet pulled, a specific conversation id, or saving a transcript by hand outside the pulse's own archiving.
routing_sentences_added.apps-registry skill description (appende: Use for editing the registry itself — for "is it running/dark", "what drifted", a newer app-registry APP also exists, see "Related app" below.
routing_sentences_added.apps-registry skill body, key line: Use the app for "which apps are running/dark right now", "what drifted from the constellation map", or any freshly-probed liveness question. Use this skill for editing apps.json itself — add/retire/archive an app, move it between families, validate/migrate the schema, or emit a shape/reshape projection.
routing_sentences_added.omi-fetch app README, key line: for "what came in" / "find the conversation about X" against the already-archived store, use this app's index (lookup.py, view/index.html) instead of the skill; for a one-off fetch of a specific date/range or conversation id not yet pulled by this app's pulse, the skill is still the right tool.
routing_sentences_added.app-registry app README, key line: For "which apps are running/dark right now" or "what drifted from the constellation map", use this app's query.py (above); for editing the registry itself (adding an app, retiring one, moving it between families), use the skill.

commits: appydave-plugins 0754317 — docs(skills): add bidirectional routing pointers for omi-fetch/apps-registry vs new dark-factory apps
omi-fetch 2a01e98 — docs: point back to the appydave:omi-fetch skill for ad-hoc fetches
app-registry 7c53483 — docs: point back to the appydave:apps-registry skill for registry CRUD
result.not_pushed: All three commits are local-only, per instruction.
result.note_for_orchestrator: Overlap with ticket 20260704T0634Z-claude-md-query-tools-pointer confirmed but NOT executed here (see files_changed.dark-factory above) — that ticket remains open in engine/store/queue/, do not mark it closed by this one.
